title: ebnerella fraileana
intro: Ebnerella fraileana, also known as the "Mountain Cinnamon" or "Cinnamon Bush," is a plant species that belongs to the Rutaceae family. It is a shrub native to the mountainous regions of Central and South America, known for its aromatic bark, leaves, and flowers, which possess medicinal properties. The bark of the Ebnerella fraileana plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of Ebnerella fraileana include teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be used to promote digestion, alleviate respiratory issues, and provide antioxidant benefits.
